---
title: "Environment"
description: "CO2 emissions, energy, and environmental indicators — Canada and OECD peers"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import os
from pipeline.config import (
    CANADA_COLOR, PEER_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH, PEER_WIDTH,
    HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY, PEER_COUNTRIES, get_data_date,
)
from pipeline.charts import peer_comparison_line
```

## CO2 Emissions per Capita

Production-based CO2 emissions per person, in tonnes of CO2. This is the most directly comparable measure of a country's carbon footprint, controlling for population size.

```{python}
#| echo: false
co2cap_path = "data/environment/oecd_co2_per_capita.csv"
if os.path.exists(co2cap_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2cap_path)

    fig = peer_comparison_line(
        df, x_col="year", y_col="co2_per_capita",
        title="", yaxis_title="Tonnes CO2 per capita",
    )
    fig.update_layout(
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2cap_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.52,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("CO2 per capita data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Latest CO2 per Capita: Ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(co2cap_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2cap_path)
    latest_year = df["year"].max()
    latest = df[df["year"] == latest_year].copy()
    latest = latest.sort_values("co2_per_capita", ascending=True)

    colors = [CANADA_COLOR if c == "CAN" else PEER_COLOR for c in latest["country_code"]]

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=latest["co2_per_capita"],
        y=latest["country_name"],
        orientation="h",
        marker_color=colors,
        hovertemplate="%{y}: %{x:.1f} t CO2/capita<extra></extra>",
    ))

    fig.update_layout(
        xaxis_title="Tonnes CO2 per capita",
        yaxis_title="",
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        showlegend=False,
        height=600,
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators ({latest_year}) | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2cap_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=1, y=-0.1,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show()
else:
    print("CO2 per capita data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## CO2 Productivity (GDP per Unit of CO2)

How much economic output is generated per unit of CO2 emitted. Higher values indicate a more carbon-efficient economy. This measures the degree to which economic growth has been decoupled from emissions.

```{python}
#| echo: false
co2int_path = "data/environment/oecd_co2_intensity.csv"
if os.path.exists(co2int_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2int_path)

    fig = peer_comparison_line(
        df, x_col="year", y_col="co2_productivity",
        title="", yaxis_title="USD per kg CO2",
    )
    fig.update_layout(
        yaxis_tickformat="$,.1f",
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2int_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.52,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("CO2 intensity data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Latest CO2 Productivity: Ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(co2int_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2int_path)
    latest_year = df["year"].max()
    latest = df[df["year"] == latest_year].copy()
    latest = latest.sort_values("co2_productivity", ascending=True)

    colors = [CANADA_COLOR if c == "CAN" else PEER_COLOR for c in latest["country_code"]]

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=latest["co2_productivity"],
        y=latest["country_name"],
        orientation="h",
        marker_color=colors,
        hovertemplate="%{y}: $%{x:.1f}/kg CO2<extra></extra>",
    ))

    fig.update_layout(
        xaxis_title="USD per kg CO2 (higher = more efficient)",
        xaxis_tickformat="$,.1f",
        yaxis_title="",
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        showlegend=False,
        height=600,
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators ({latest_year}) | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2int_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=1, y=-0.1,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show()
else:
    print("CO2 intensity data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## CO2 Emissions Trend (Indexed, 2000 = 100)

How each country's CO2 emissions have changed relative to 2000 levels. Values below 100 indicate reductions since 2000; above 100 indicate growth.

```{python}
#| echo: false
co2idx_path = "data/environment/oecd_co2_indexed.csv"
if os.path.exists(co2idx_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2idx_path)

    fig = peer_comparison_line(
        df, x_col="year", y_col="co2_index",
        title="", yaxis_title="Index (2000 = 100)",
    )
    # Add reference line at 100
    fig.add_hline(y=100, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888", opacity=0.5)
    fig.update_layout(
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2idx_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.52,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("CO2 indexed data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Renewable Energy Share

Renewable energy as a percentage of total primary energy supply. Canada has significant hydroelectric capacity, which shows in its renewable share.

```{python}
#| echo: false
renew_path = "data/environment/oecd_renewables_share.csv"
if os.path.exists(renew_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(renew_path)

    fig = peer_comparison_line(
        df, x_col="year", y_col="renewables_pct",
        title="", yaxis_title="% of total energy supply",
    )
    fig.update_layout(
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(renew_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.52,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Renewable energy data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Latest Renewable Energy Share: Ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(renew_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(renew_path)
    latest_year = df["year"].max()
    latest = df[df["year"] == latest_year].copy()
    latest = latest.sort_values("renewables_pct", ascending=True)

    colors = [CANADA_COLOR if c == "CAN" else PEER_COLOR for c in latest["country_code"]]

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=latest["renewables_pct"],
        y=latest["country_name"],
        orientation="h",
        marker_color=colors,
        hovertemplate="%{y}: %{x:.1f}%<extra></extra>",
    ))

    fig.update_layout(
        xaxis_title="% of total energy supply",
        yaxis_title="",
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        showlegend=False,
        height=600,
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators ({latest_year}) | Data as of: {get_data_date(renew_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=1, y=-0.1,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show()
else:
    print("Renewable energy data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Download Data

- [CO2 per Capita (CSV)](../data/environment/oecd_co2_per_capita.csv) — OECD Green Growth Indicators
- [CO2 Productivity (CSV)](../data/environment/oecd_co2_intensity.csv) — OECD Green Growth Indicators
- [CO2 Emissions Indexed (CSV)](../data/environment/oecd_co2_indexed.csv) — OECD Green Growth Indicators
- [Renewable Energy Share (CSV)](../data/environment/oecd_renewables_share.csv) — OECD Green Growth Indicators
